title: F1-tests: config panel coverage — uninit error, atomic write, per-field roundtrip, validation, exit, install regression
description: Add comprehensive vitest coverage for the new config panel. Required test cases: (1) uninit error path (fabric.config.json absent → error msg + 'fab install' hint + non-zero exit); (2) atomic write — verify no `.tmp` file remains after edit; (3) per-field roundtrip — for each Group A/B/C field, simulate edit + verify on-disk JSON reflects change; (4) validation rejection — feed negative integer to a Group B threshold, verify rejection with localized error message; (5) exit path — choose exit, verify clean outro and no writes; (6) installMcpClients export anti-regression — re-import via `import * as configCommand` and verify the named export still exists with the same signature; (7) i18n smoke — set fabric_language to 'zh-CN' in pre-existing config, run panel, verify menu labels are Chinese.

files.change: New test file with describe blocks: 'uninit handling', 'atomic write', 'field edit roundtrip', 'validation', 'exit', 'install export regression', 'i18n menu localization'

implementation: Set up tmp workspace fixture per test (mkdtempSync + cleanup in afterEach)
For uninit test: create tmp workspace WITHOUT fabric.config.json, invoke configCmd.run, capture stderr/exit code, assert message contains both 'fab install' substring AND localized error key
For atomic write test: pre-populate fabric.config.json, mock clack prompts to choose+edit a field, run, then assert (a) fabric.config.json updated, (b) no fabric.config.json.tmp exists
For per-field roundtrip: parametrized test over getPanelFields() — each iteration edits one field, asserts that field changed and ALL other fields preserved (anti-regression for spread-merge)
For validation: mock prompt response with invalid value, assert validate() rejects with t('cli.config.invalid.input') message
For exit: mock prompt to immediately select __exit__, assert no writes occurred and outro called

For install export regression: `import * as configCommand from '../src/commands/config.js'` + assert `typeof configCommand.installMcpClients === 'function'` + assert function arity matches (target + options)
Use clack-prompts test utilities (or stub @clack/prompts module via vi.mock) to programmatically drive the menu

reference.pattern: Existing command tests under packages/cli/test/commands/ (find via glob); install.test.ts likely uses similar tmp-workspace + clack-mock pattern
reference.examples: install.test.ts demonstrates clack mocking + tmp workspace fixture — copy the harness

criteria: File `packages/cli/test/commands/config.test.ts` exists with at least 7 test groups covering all listed scenarios
All 11 panel fields covered by parametrized roundtrip test (proves no field is silently broken)
Anti-regression: install.test.ts and any test importing configCommand.installMcpClients still passes (named-export contract preserved)
Test for `fabric_language: 'zh-CN'` localization PASSES — proves the panel honors fabric_language not just node locale
Behavioral expectation: any future regression in config.ts panel logic (broken atomic write, missing field, validation bypass) is caught by this suite at PR time

rationale.chosen_approach: Single dedicated test file with explicit scenario describe blocks — matches existing command-test convention and keeps the panel contract auditable
alternatives_considered: Snapshot tests for the rendered menu (rejected: clack output is hard to snapshot stably; substring assertions are more robust)
Integration test via subprocess spawn (rejected: 10x slower than vitest unit tests; reserve for end-to-end smoke)
decision_factors: Each Group A/B/C field is user-visible — silent regression here means broken UX
installMcpClients named-export is a hidden contract — explicit anti-regression test makes it visible
rationale.tradeoffs: Mocking @clack/prompts requires test setup boilerplate; offset by reusing the install.test.ts harness

manual_checks: Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- config.test` and inspect coverage report
Intentionally break the atomic-write logic in config.ts (skip the rename) and confirm the test catches it
success_metrics: 11/11 panel fields covered by roundtrip test
